title: Vehicle Stability Assist (VSA) System Structure Description - EBD Control

blocks.text: Vehicle Stability Assist (VSA) System Structure Description - EBD Control

In order to effectively transmit brake force to the road surface during braking, EBD control appropriately controls the distribution of front and rear brake force in conjunction with load shifting due to loading conditions and deceleration, and controls rear wheel brake pressure to ensure optimal rear wheel brake force according to vehicle conditions.

When braking at deceleration above a certain value, the inlet valve closes and holds brake pressure when the rear wheel speed decreases relative to the estimated vehicle body speed. When wheel speed recovers, the inlet valve opens momentarily to increase brake pressure. When pressure reduction is necessary, the outlet valve opens momentarily and the pump motor is activated to reduce brake pressure. These operations are quickly repeated for EBD control.
